locations.name: Hotel EMC2
locations.note: Home base. Art-and-science boutique hotel in Streeterville, steps from the Mag Mile and Navy Pier.

locations.name: Field Museum (Pokémon Fossil Museum)
locations.note: Pokémon Fossil exhibit runs through April 2027. Opens 9am-5pm. ~$33. Book ahead.

locations.name: Cloud Gate (The Bean)
locations.note: Iconic mirror sculpture in Millennium Park. Free. Busiest midday — go early or evening.

locations.name: Obama Presidential Center
locations.note: Opens 10am Sat. ~7.5mi south of home base. No. 10 express bus goes direct. Book at obama.org.

locations.name: Chicago Riverwalk
locations.note: Walkable path along the river. Bars, cafes, boat tours. Beautiful at night.

locations.name: Grant Park
locations.note: Large downtown park connecting the Museum Campus to Millennium Park. Home to the Grant Park Music Festival.

locations.name: Navy Pier
locations.note: Ferris wheel, boat rides, food. Touristy but fun. Walkable from home base.

locations.name: Dusty Groove
locations.note: Famous record store. 9am-7pm daily. Deep soul, funk, jazz, and Latin vinyl.

locations.name: Rooftop Cinema Club
locations.note: Open-air rooftop movies in Fulton Market. Wireless headphones, deck chairs, bar.

locations.name: Trump Tower
locations.note: Riverside skyscraper, photo stop along the Riverwalk.

locations.name: Lincoln Park Zoo
locations.note: Free zoo in Lincoln Park. 8am-5pm (later in summer). Easy to combine with the conservatory.

locations.name: Lincoln Park Conservatory
locations.note: Free Victorian glass-house conservatory. Wed-Sun 10am-4:30pm.

locations.name: Art Institute of Chicago
locations.note: World-class art museum. Closed Tuesdays. Allow 2-3 hours.

locations.name: Magnificent Mile
locations.note: Michigan Ave shopping corridor — flagship stores, hotels, restaurants.

locations.name: Shedd Aquarium
locations.note: On the Museum Campus next to the Field Museum. ~$50. Book ahead in summer.

locations.name: University of Chicago
locations.note: Gothic campus on the South Side, near the Obama Center. Good for a walk-around.

locations.name: Donda's House (Kanye childhood home)
locations.note: Exterior only — private residence on the South Side. A fan photo stop, ~8mi from home base.

locations.name: North Avenue Beach
locations.note: Lakefront beach with a boathouse bar. Volleyball, skyline views.

locations.name: Wicker Park
locations.note: Hip neighborhood — vintage shops, bars, music venues, cafes.

locations.name: Garfield Park Conservatory
locations.note: One of the largest conservatories in the US. 4.8 stars. ~$10 for visitors.

locations.name: The Chicago Theatre
locations.note: Iconic 1921 landmark marquee. Concerts, comedy, live shows. Check what's on for your dates.

locations.name: Gold Coast
locations.note: Upscale neighborhood — boutiques, Rush Street nightlife, historic mansions.

locations.name: Fulton Market District
locations.note: Former meatpacking district, now Chicago's hottest dining and nightlife strip.

locations.name: Chinatown Square Plaza
locations.note: Two-level outdoor mall — the heart of Chinatown. Restaurants, bubble tea, bakeries.

locations.name: Pizzeria Uno
locations.note: The original birthplace of Chicago deep dish (1943). Near home base.

locations.name: Lou Malnati's (River North)
locations.note: Legendary buttercrust deep dish. A local favorite.

locations.name: Gino's East
locations.note: Famous for graffiti-covered walls — write on them. Classic deep dish.

locations.name: Pequod's Pizza
locations.note: Cult favorite. Caramelized cheese crust 'halo'. Lincoln Park.

locations.name: Bartoli's Pizzeria
locations.note: Award-winning, BYOB, underrated local pick near Wrigleyville.

locations.name: Chicago Pizza & Oven Grinder Co.
locations.note: Across from the St. Valentine's Day Massacre site. Famous upside-down pizza pot pie.

locations.name: Portillo's
locations.note: The gold standard Chicago dog and Italian beef. 2 blocks from home base. Open till 1am.

locations.name: The Wiener's Circle
locations.note: Char dogs and famously sassy late-night staff. Open till 4am Fri/Sat — great post-club stop.

locations.name: Jim's Original
locations.note: Since 1939. Maxwell Street Polish sausage — a piece of Chicago history.

locations.name: Mr. Beef
locations.note: The Italian beef spot that inspired The Bear. Go earlier — lines form. A must.

locations.name: Chicago Chop House
locations.note: Fine-dining steakhouse, 4.7 stars. Walking distance from home base. 4-10pm daily.

locations.name: Molly Tea
locations.note: Popular bubble tea spot in Chinatown on Wentworth.

locations.name: Postcard Cafe
locations.note: Aesthetic Fulton Market cafe — matcha lattes. 7am-7pm daily.

locations.name: Levain Bakery
locations.note: Famous oversized cookies. 7:30am-11pm daily. Fulton Market.

locations.name: Gao's BBQ & Crab
locations.note: Chinese BBQ skewers and crab near Chinatown. Open till 2am — great late-night food.

locations.name: Sound Bar
locations.note: Multi-room club, big sound system. 2 blocks from home base. Fri/Sat 10pm-4/5am.

locations.name: Spy Bar
locations.note: Hot spot. Intimate underground club, house and techno. River North. 5-min walk from home base.

locations.name: Kashmir
locations.note: West Loop club, ~15-min Uber. Fri/Sat 9pm-2/3am.

locations.name: Smart Bar
locations.note: Legendary house/techno club below Metro in Wrigleyville. ~4mi north.

locations.name: Beauty Bar
locations.note: Dance club in a retro salon setting, West Town. ~2.5mi west.

locations.name: Cara Cara Club
locations.note: Logan Square spot. Fri/Sat 5pm-1am. ~5mi northwest.

locations.name: Podlasie Club
locations.note: Avondale dance club. ~6mi northwest — the farthest of the nightlife picks.

locations.name: Smoke & Mirrors
locations.note: Logan Square club. Fri/Sat 10pm-4/5am.

locations.name: Radius Chicago
locations.note: Large live-music and DJ venue near Chinatown, by the Cermak-Chinatown stop.

locations.name: Soldier Field
locations.note: Chicago Bears and major concerts. Next to the Field Museum and Shedd.

locations.name: Wrigley Field
locations.note: Iconic 1914 Cubs ballpark. The Wrigleyville bar scene around it is worth it even without a game.

locations.name: Guaranteed Rate Field
locations.note: Chicago White Sox. Red Line to Sox/35th. Near Chinatown.

locations.name: United Center
locations.note: Bulls and Blackhawks, plus major concerts. ~2.5mi west.

locations.name: Wintrust Arena
locations.note: Smaller arena — DePaul basketball and concerts. Near Chinatown/South Loop.

locations.name: Green Mill Cocktail Lounge
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. Capone's favorite booth still there. Live jazz nightly. Uptown, ~5.5mi north.

locations.name: Green Door Tavern
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. Original Prohibition speakeasy with a basement bar (The Drifter). Steps from home base.

locations.name: St. Valentine's Day Massacre Site
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. Empty lot where 7 men were gunned down in 1929. Historical marker only.

locations.name: Harry Caray's Italian Steakhouse
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. Frank Nitti's former apartment; hidden tunnel and vault in the basement. Near home base.

locations.name: Chicago History Museum
locations.note: Best Prohibition and Capone exhibits in the city. Closed Mondays.

locations.name: Holy Name Cathedral
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. 1926 bullet holes from the Hymie Weiss hit once visible outside. One block from home base.

locations.name: Chicago Gangsters & Ghosts Tour
locations.note: 4.9-star walking tour covering the major mob sites. A good one-stop overview.

locations.name: Biograph Theater
locations.note: 1920s/30s gangster site. John Dillinger was shot by FBI agents in the alley here in 1934. Mural marks the spot.

locations.name: Al Capone's Chicago Manor
locations.note: 1920s gangster site. Capone's actual family home — exterior only, sealed escape tunnels. ~8mi south.

locations.name: Chicago Crime Tours
locations.note: 4.8-star bus tour of gangster sites. 2 blocks from home base.

locations.name: Cermak-Chinatown CTA
locations.note: Red Line stop — the direct shot south to Chinatown from downtown.

locations.name: Chicago/State CTA
locations.note: Red Line stop nearest to home base — one block away.

locations.name: No. 10 Bus — Obama Express
locations.note: Downtown stop for the No. 10 express bus to the Museum Campus and Obama Center.
